core_mechanism: A uniform asymptotic (WKB / Liouville-Green, 'Olver'-type) transformation that maps one second-order linear ODE onto a simpler solvable reference equation by matching their phase integrals (actions) through a turning point, yielding an approximation that stays smooth and uniformly valid across the turning point and collapses exactly to the reference solution in the appropriate limit.
brief_reason: The abstract states the central development is a 'leading-order Olver construction' that 'maps the curved radial equation onto the flat spherical Bessel equation by matching their actions through the turning point,' remaining 'exact in the flat limit' and 'smooth through the turning point' — a turning-point-matched uniform asymptotic reduction of one ODE to another.
